subject: Geography
title: The Canary Islands
description: Learn The Canary Islands in one minute.

hook.text: The Canary Islands sit off northwest Africa but belong to Spain, 1,000 km from Madrid.

buildup.text: They formed from a volcanic hotspot, similar to Hawaii, over millions of years.

discovery.text: Tenerife's Mount Teide is the tallest peak in Spain—higher than anything on the mainland.

twist.text: A future eruption on La Palma could trigger a mega-tsunami crossing the Atlantic.

climax.text: La Palma erupted in 2021, destroying 3,000 buildings as lava reached the sea.

punchline.text: Spain's tallest mountain isn't in Spain—it's in Africa's shadow.

quiz.question: Where are the Canary Islands located?
options: Off the northwest coast of Africa
